pr.title: Match several ft, pl, and if functions
pr.author: itsgrimetime

summary: Matched several previously nonmatching functions across fighter common guard logic, player attack stats reset logic, and interface/UI code. The PR explicitly matched ftCo_80091BC4, ftCo_80092158, ftCo_80093790, plAttack_8003759C, and un_802FE260, and improved fn_802FDA78 using file-local struct typing. The changes mainly replaced m2c-style temporaries, helper inlines, and overly broad direct global accesses with clearer typed locals, pointer aliases, signed sentinel typing, and small helper functions that still preserve matching.

key_files.role: Matched ftCo_80091BC4, ftCo_80092158, and ftCo_80093790 by restructuring guard angle smoothing, color packing for efSync_Spawn, and shield/reflect side effects.
Matched un_802FE260 and improved fn_802FDA78 through local struct pointer aliasing, signed sentinel typing for x31, const slot table data, and more explicit material color component writes.
Matched plAttack_8003759C by extracting repeated attack-stat reset code into a static inline clearAttackStats helper and adjusting loop bounds.

what_changed: ftCo_80091BC4 was rewritten from nested blocks and normalizeAngle180/normalizeAngle0 helper inlines into a flatter sequence using named locals: stick_rad, stick_deg, deg_delta, guard_deg, smoothed_deg, and stick_mag.

The guard angle update now performs the angle wrapping inline: stick radians are normalized to [0, 2π), stick degrees are clamped to 0..359, delta is wrapped around ±180, and smoothed guard degrees are wrapped around 0..360 before storing fp->mv.co.guard.x8.
ftCo_80091BC4 now updates guard magnitude with fp->mv.co.guard.x4 += p_ftCommonData->x44C * (stick_mag - fp->mv.co.guard.x4), replacing an equivalent but less readable temporary-heavy expression.
ftCo_80092158 replaced M2C_FIELD and M2C_BITWISE-style byte extraction with an offset into Fighter_804D650C based on Player_GetUnk45(... ) << 2 and direct color[0], color[1], color[2] indexing.
ftCo_80092158 simplified efSync_Spawn arguments to a packed RGB integer: (color[0] << 16) | (color[1] << 8) | color[2].

ftCo_80093790 was flattened by directly calling ftCo_80092158 with fp->parts[fp->ft_data->x8->x11].joint, setting fp->x2219_b0, and playing SFX. A local zeroed u8 padding array was introduced, likely for stack/layout matching.
if_2FC93 changed struct field x31 from unsigned char to signed char so -1 can be used naturally as the sentinel instead of 0xFF.
if_2FC93 made un_804DDBA8 a static const char array and casts its addresses to void* when stored as gobj->user_data.
fn_802FDA78 now aliases &un_804A1F10 through a local struct un_804A1F10_t* p and uses p->field accesses throughout much of the function.
fn_802FDA78 changed material diffuse assignment from whole HSD_Color assignment to explicit r, g, b, a component writes for each of three material objects.
fn_802FDA78 changed j and k counters to u8 locals initialized once near the top, uses p->x31 == -1 checks, and uses 2.6f float literals in translate calculations.
un_802FE260 now uses the same local struct pointer alias p for x24, x28, and x4 initialization after creating UI GObjs and loading JObjs.
plAttack_8003759C now uses a static inline clearAttackStats(struct plAttackStats* s) helper to reset attacks, hits, and x358_hits.

plAttack_8003759C and clearAttackStats reset attack-count arrays from index 0 to StatsAttack_Count instead of the previous excerpted loops starting at index 2, and x504 is likewise cleared from index 0.
smart_moves: Using a file-local pointer alias, struct un_804A1F10_t* p = &un_804A1F10, reduced repeated global access noise and appears to have helped both fn_802FDA78 and un_802FE260 match while making stateful UI code easier to read.
Changing x31 to signed char made the sentinel value -1 explicit and avoided treating 0xFF as an unsigned magic value throughout the UI state logic.
Packing Fighter_804D650C color bytes with direct array indexing removed M2C_FIELD/M2C_BITWISE artifacts and clarified that the data is RGB-like color data.

Inlining the angle normalization logic in ftCo_80091BC4 with carefully ordered temporaries preserved matching while documenting the guard stick-angle smoothing behavior more directly than generic normalizeAngle helpers.
Extracting clearAttackStats avoided triplicated reset logic in plAttack_8003759C while still matching, showing that small static inline helpers can be viable when compiler output cooperates.
The explicit component-wise diffuse color writes in fn_802FDA78 are a useful matching tactic when whole-struct color assignments do not produce the desired codegen.
The PR included build verification commands: python configure.py --require-protos, ninja build/GALE01/report.json, and ninja.
decomp_lessons: For matching, clearer high-level code can replace m2c artifacts if local variable lifetimes, signedness, and expression grouping are chosen carefully.
Sentinel fields should be typed to match their semantic use. If code checks for -1, a signed char field can produce cleaner source and better matching than unsigned char plus 0xFF.
When a global struct is accessed repeatedly in a function, introducing a local pointer alias can affect codegen and often improves readability without losing evidence about the underlying storage.
Whole-struct assignment is not always the best decomp representation for small POD-like fields; explicit per-channel assignments may better match generated stores.

Loop bounds are part of the matching signal. This PR changed player attack-stat resets from index 2-based loops to index 0-based loops, so future work should verify reset ranges against both behavior and matching output.
Small static inline reset helpers can reduce repetition in matched code, but should be validated with report.json because helper extraction can change inlining and stack behavior.
Color-table lookups that appear as M2C_FIELD byte accesses may often be reconstructed as byte-array indexing plus bit packing.
Padding locals such as the zeroed u8 array in ftCo_80093790 may be necessary for stack or register-layout matching; keep them evidence-labeled rather than over-explaining their purpose.
naming_conventions: Kept existing decomp placeholder names for unmatched or unknown symbols, including ftCo_80091BC4, ftCo_80092158, ftCo_80093790, fn_802FDA78, un_802FE260, un_804A1F10, un_804DDBA8, and un_804D6D90.
Used descriptive local names in ftCo_80091BC4 such as stick_rad, stick_deg, deg_delta, guard_deg, smoothed_deg, and stick_mag to expose guard-angle smoothing semantics.
Used color as the local pointer name for Fighter_804D650C byte data in ftCo_80092158, reflecting its use in efSync_Spawn RGB packing.
Used clearAttackStats as a static inline helper name for resetting struct plAttackStats fields.
Used short local pointer alias p for the file-local un_804A1F10 state struct in if_2FC93.c.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed M2C_FIELD and M2C_BITWISE artifacts from ftCo_80092158 by replacing them with direct byte pointer arithmetic and RGB bit packing.
Used an offset computed as Player_GetUnk45(GET_FIGHTER(gobj)->player_id) << 2 to index Fighter_804D650C.
Replaced normalizeAngle180 and normalizeAngle0 helper inlines with explicit branch-based wrapping in ftCo_80091BC4, likely to control instruction ordering and temporary lifetimes.
Introduced a zero-initialized u8 _[8] local in ftCo_80093790, likely a stack-layout matching tactic based on the diff, though the slice does not explain it.
Changed HSD material diffuse assignment to explicit r/g/b/a member stores in fn_802FDA78.
Used signed char x31 and -1 comparisons instead of unsigned char x31 and 0xFF comparisons.
Used 2.6f float literals in HSD_JObjSetTranslateY expressions to control float constant/codegen behavior.
Used a static inline helper clearAttackStats to factor repeated reset code while preserving a match.
Casted &un_804DDBA8[i] to void* for gobj->user_data assignment after making un_804DDBA8 const.

review_feedback.text: The slice records one review and no review comments; no specific reviewer feedback is available in the provided context.
PR body listed verification with python configure.py --require-protos, ninja build/GALE01/report.json, and ninja.

searchable_terms: Match several ft, pl, and if functions
follow_up_queries: Find other M2C_FIELD color-table byte extraction sites that can be rewritten as typed byte indexing and RGB packing like ftCo_80092158.
Search for unsigned char fields compared against 0xFF that may be better represented as signed char sentinel fields.
Look for repeated global struct access in if/UI files where a local pointer alias may improve matching and readability.
Audit player stats reset functions for loop-bound differences between index 0 and index 2 to ensure source matches game behavior and compiler output.
Search for whole HSD color or material struct assignments that may need explicit component writes for matching.
